events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario is about roofing work with "insufficient overhang" as the observed field condition. I need to estimate the measurable condition and judge whether it's within tolerance.

Insufficient overhang on roofing - this relates to drip edge and underlayment overhang requirements. IRC R905 and manufacturer specs typically require a minimum overhang (often 1/4" to 1/2" for drip edge, and underlayment should overhang at least 1/4" but not more than 1/2" typically). 

The condition is "insufficient overhang" which means the roofing material/drip edge/underlayment doesn't extend far enough over the edge. This is a defect - water can back up under the roofing, leading to water damage.

This is a fail condition - non-compliant with code and manufacturer specs.

    "Roofing drip edge / underlayment overhang is insufficient at eaves and/or rakes — does not meet minimum projection required by IRC R905 and manufacturer installation instructions",
    "Insufficient overhang risks water wicking back under roofing and rotting fascia/edge trim"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove and re-install drip edge and/or starter course to achieve required overhang per mfr specs (typically 1/4–1/2 in. beyond fascia)",
    "Verify underlayment extends over drip edge per IRC R905.2.7 and manufacturer requirements",
    "Re-inspect edge detailing before closing out roof"
  ],
  "rationale": "Insufficient overhang at roof edges is a non-compliant installation defect. IRC R905 and manufacturer specs require drip edge and underlayment to project a minimum distance over the fascia/rake to direct water off the roof edge and prevent wind-driven water from migrating back under the roofing. At rough-complete stage this must be corrected before closeout.",
  "references": ["IRC R905", "IRC R905.2.7", "mfr specs"]
}
